_meta.name: Rattlesnake Security Enforcement System
_meta.description: Security pattern registry — headers, middleware recipes, validation patterns, scan patterns. Injected into model context via _build_security_context(). The WiringAgent scanner uses security_scan_patterns to detect vulnerabilities post-scaffold.
_meta.usage: Loaded by _load_security_json(). Context built by _build_security_context(). Scan patterns compiled by _get_security_scan_patterns().

philosophy: Security is not optional. Every project gets security headers, input validation, and auth checks by default.
Defense in depth: multiple layers (headers, middleware, validation, CSP) so one failure doesn't expose everything.
Secrets never touch client code. Server secrets stay server-side. Environment variables are the only store.
Validate at the boundary. Every request body, query param, and URL param is validated before processing.
Fail closed. If auth check fails, return 401. If validation fails, return 400. Never proceed with bad data.
Least privilege. Users see only their own data. RLS + application-level user_id filtering.
No security theater. Don't add deprecated headers (X-XSS-Protection). Use standards that actually work (CSP).
Scan what you mandate. If the system prompt says 'use CORS correctly', the scanner must verify it.
security_rules: Every POST/PUT/PATCH handler MUST validate request body before processing
Every GET handler with query params MUST validate/sanitize parameters
Every protected API route MUST call getUser() or getSession() at the start
Return 401 for missing/invalid auth, 400 for validation failure, 404 for not found, 500 for server errors
NEVER expose stack traces, internal errors, or database errors to users
All secrets stored in .env — NEVER hardcoded in source files
Server secrets MUST NOT use NEXT_PUBLIC_, REACT_APP_, VITE_, or NUXT_PUBLIC_ prefixes
.env.example MUST exist with placeholder values (no real secrets)
next.config.js MUST include security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, CSP, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
CSP MUST NOT include 'unsafe-eval' unless specifically required (e.g., certain MDX plugins)
NEVER use eval() or new Function() in application code
NEVER use innerHTML or dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify sanitization
NEVER store JWT/auth tokens in localStorage — use httpOnly cookies
NEVER use origin: '*' in CORS configuration for production APIs
NEVER redirect users based on unvalidated user input (req.query, searchParams)
All cookies MUST set httpOnly, secure, and sameSite attributes
File uploads MUST validate file type, size, and content before processing
All text inputs MUST have maxLength to prevent payload abuse
Database queries MUST use parameterized queries — NEVER string interpolation
Wrap all database operations in try/catch with safe error responses
Filter all data queries by user_id to prevent cross-user data leakage
Rate limit authentication endpoints (login, signup, password reset)
Use CSRF protection on all state-changing operations
Password fields MUST NOT store cleartext — always hash with bcrypt/argon2/scrypt
API keys in code MUST come from environment variables, not string literals

nextjs.description: Security headers for next.config.js headers() function. Inject into async headers() return value.

headers.description: Prevents MIME-type sniffing. Browser trusts declared Content-Type.
Prevents clickjacking by blocking iframe embedding. Use SAMEORIGIN if you need iframe embeds from same domain.
Sends full URL for same-origin, only origin for cross-origin. Balances analytics needs with privacy.
Disables browser APIs not needed by most web apps. Add back specific permissions if your app needs them.
Forces HTTPS for 1 year. WARNING: Do NOT enable on localhost — HSTS on plain HTTP will lock out local development. Next.js dev server uses HTTP by default. Enable only in production with HTTPS.

headers.value: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'self' https://*.supabase.co
headers.description: Controls which resources the browser can load. NO 'unsafe-eval' — it re-enables eval()/new Function(). Add 'unsafe-eval' to script-src ONLY if your setup specifically requires it (e.g., certain MDX plugins).

html.description: CSP as meta tag for static HTML. This is the only security header that works reliably as a meta tag. X-Frame-Options and X-Content-Type-Options don't work as meta tags — they're HTTP response headers only.
html.meta_tag: <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://cdn.tailwindcss.com 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com;">

express.description: Security headers for Express/Fastify/Hono via helmet middleware. Install: npm install helmet

headers.description: Forces HTTPS. Enable only in production.

generic.description: Generic security headers applicable to any HTTP server. Apply via middleware or reverse proxy.

rate_limiter.name: Rate Limiter
rate_limiter.description: Token bucket rate limiting for auth endpoints. Prevents brute-force attacks on login/signup/password-reset.

auth_guard.name: Auth Guard
auth_guard.description: Supabase auth check for protected API routes. Call at the start of every handler that needs authentication.

cors_config.name: CORS Configuration
cors_config.description: Properly configured CORS headers. NEVER use origin: '*' in production — always whitelist specific origins.

input_sanitizer.name: Input Sanitizer
input_sanitizer.description: Validation utility for request bodies. Validates types, lengths, required fields before processing.

csrf_protection.name: CSRF Protection
csrf_protection.description: CSRF token validation for state-changing requests. Uses double-submit cookie pattern.

password.name: Password strength
rules: At least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit

url.name: URL validation
rules: Must start with http:// or https://

extra_rules: Validate payment amounts server-side — never trust client-side price
PCI compliance: never log or store raw card numbers
Use Stripe/payment processor tokens, not raw card data

api.description: API/backend services — auth + rate limiting + CORS

security_scan_patterns.description: Regex-based vulnerability scan patterns used by _scan_security_quality(). Each pattern has a regex, severity, message, and optional exceptions.

patterns.name: sql_injection
patterns.message: Possible SQL injection: use parameterized queries instead of string interpolation in database calls

patterns.name: missing_auth_check
patterns.description: API handlers without auth check. Only fires when no middleware.ts/middleware.js exists in project root.
patterns.message: API handler may be missing authentication check — call getUser()/getSession() at start of handler

patterns.name: wildcard_cors
patterns.message: Wildcard CORS (origin: '*') allows any website to make requests to your API — whitelist specific origins

patterns.name: unvalidated_redirect
patterns.message: Redirect from unvalidated user input — validate/whitelist URLs before redirecting

patterns.name: cleartext_password
patterns.message: Possible cleartext password literal — use bcrypt/argon2/scrypt hashing

patterns.name: missing_rate_limit
patterns.description: Auth POST handlers without rate limiting. Only fires on security-aware projects (migration path).
patterns.message: Auth endpoint missing rate limiting — add rate limiter to prevent brute-force attacks

patterns.name: exposed_server_secret
patterns.message: Server secret exposed with client-side environment prefix — remove NEXT_PUBLIC_/REACT_APP_/VITE_/NUXT_PUBLIC_ prefix

patterns.name: innerHTML_xss
patterns.message: innerHTML/dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization — use DOMPurify.sanitize() to prevent XSS

patterns.name: eval_usage
patterns.message: eval() or new Function() enables arbitrary code execution — find a safer alternative

patterns.name: missing_input_length
patterns.message: Text input without maxLength — add maxLength to prevent payload abuse

patterns.name: jwt_in_localstorage
patterns.message: Auth token stored in localStorage — use httpOnly cookies instead (localStorage is accessible to XSS)

patterns.name: hardcoded_api_key
patterns.message: Hardcoded API key — move to .env and access via process.env

patterns.name: unvalidated_file_upload
patterns.message: File upload without type/size validation — validate file type, size, and content before processing

patterns.name: insecure_cookie
patterns.message: Cookie set without security attributes — add httpOnly, secure, and sameSite flags
